openQuestions.text: Q1: What replaces next-saas-stripe-starter as the dashboard boilerplate? (dead repo — find living alternatives with Next.js 16 + App Router)
Q2: Next.js 16 — what's new, stable?, breaking changes from 14? Migration path?
Q3: Prisma 7 — stable? Breaking changes from 5.x? New features relevant to viral-ops?
Q4: n8n 2.x — stable? Breaking changes from 1.x? New features? Self-hosted licensing changes?
Q5: Auth.js / NextAuth — latest stable version? Breaking changes? Better alternatives?
Q6: ShadCN UI + Tremor + Tailwind CSS — latest versions? Tailwind v4? Tremor status?
Q7: Pixelle-Video — latest version? API changes since v0.1.15? New features?
Q8: Node.js LTS, PostgreSQL, TypeScript — latest stable versions to pin?
Q9: ComfyUI, Edge-TTS, RunningHub — any significant updates?
Q10: Does the gen1 architecture (3-service localhost) still hold with updated versions? Any breaking incompatibilities?

keyFindings.text: **Assessment**: HIGH RISK. Tremor appears to have very low development activity. Zero GitHub releases, tremor-raw abandoned and merged back. The 84-commit count from iteration 1 combined with zero releases strongly suggests the project is stalled or has shifted to a different publishing model. Tailwind v4 compatibility is UNCONFIRMED and likely absent.
sources: https://github.com/t3-oss/create-t3-app (fetched: stars, releases, tech stack, features)

keyFindings.text: **Assessment**: SECONDARY. Very popular but scaffold-only (no batteries), no payments, maintenance gap. Good for typesafety focus (tRPC) but needs significant additions.
sources: https://github.com/t3-oss/create-t3-app (fetched: stars, releases, tech stack, features)

keyFindings.text: **Auth.js has merged into / been absorbed by Better Auth** -- The authjs.dev homepage now states "The Auth.js project is now part of Better Auth." This is a significant change from gen1 where Auth.js v5 was planned. Need to evaluate Better Auth as replacement or decide whether to use next-forge's built-in Clerk auth instead. [SOURCE: https://authjs.dev]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: **next-forge monorepo structure uses workspace packages for auth, design-system, CMS, email, analytics** -- Auth is in `@repo/auth` (not directly Clerk in web app), design system in `@repo/design-system` (likely wraps shadcn/ui), Prisma presumably in a database package. No Prisma or Clerk directly in `apps/web/package.json`. Uses Arcjet for security, Fumadocs for docs, MDX bundler. [SOURCE: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/haydenbleasel/next-forge/main/apps/web/package.json]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: **next-forge uses Bun 1.3.10 as package manager** -- Root package.json specifies `packageManager: bun@1.3.10`. This is notable since gen1 assumed npm/pnpm. Bun is compatible with Node.js 18+ (engine requirement). [SOURCE: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/haydenbleasel/next-forge/main/package.json]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: **next-forge v6.0.2 uses Next.js 16.1.6, Tailwind CSS 4.2.1, React 19.2.4** -- The root package.json is a Turborepo monorepo; actual framework deps live in `apps/web/package.json`. Confirmed: Next.js 16.1.6 (not 15.x), Tailwind CSS 4.2.1 (v4, not v3), React 19.2.4. Also uses Zod 4.3.6, Sentry 10.42.0, date-fns 4.1.0, sharp 0.34.5. TypeScript ^5.9.3 pinned in root devDependencies. [SOURCE: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/haydenbleasel/next-forge/main/apps/web/package.json]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: **PostgreSQL 18.3 is the latest stable (Feb 2026)** -- PG 18 is the current major version, not PG 17 as expected. Supported versions: 18.3, 17.9, 16.13, 15.17, 14.22. Gen1 planned PG 16.x; upgrading to PG 17.x or 18.x is recommended. Prisma 7.4 compatibility with PG 18 needs verification but Prisma typically supports latest PG within weeks of release. [SOURCE: https://www.postgresql.org/]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: **shadcn/ui has full chart support built on Recharts v3** -- Six chart types available: Bar, Line, Area, Pie, Radar, Radial. Custom components: `ChartContainer`, `ChartTooltip`, `ChartTooltipContent`, `ChartLegend`, `ChartLegendContent`. Uses configuration-based `ChartConfig` system. Install via `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add chart`. Does NOT wrap Recharts -- allows direct use of Recharts components with optional custom wrappers. Sufficient for analytics dashboard (covers all chart types needed). [SOURCE: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: **TypeScript 6.0 is the current stable release** -- Major version jump from 5.x to 6.0. next-forge pins ^5.9.3 in root devDeps (compatible with 5.9.x but not auto-upgrading to 6.0). Viral-ops should decide: pin 5.9.x for stability with next-forge, or upgrade to 6.0 (may require next-forge compatibility check). [SOURCE: https://www.typescriptlang.org/download/]
sources: https://authjs.dev (Auth.js homepage -- Better Auth merger notice)

keyFindings.text: Active development: commits through Jan 2026 including action migration module (Jan 26) and digital human anchor feature (Jan 14)
A "v2.0 Migration tool" exists for automated migration
**Better Auth escape hatch**: If Clerk pricing becomes prohibitive at scale or vendor lock-in is unacceptable:
Better Auth has a Prisma adapter (compatible with our PostgreSQL + Prisma 7 stack)
Better Auth is the spiritual successor to Auth.js (though no formal absorption confirmed in their docs)
Both are core Pixelle-Video dependencies, actively maintained within that ecosystem
**Bun vs Node conflict?**: No -- Bun is only for the next-forge monorepo; n8n runs in its own Node.js process
Clerk free tier supports 10K MAUs -- sufficient for viral-ops MVP/dev phase
Clerk provides managed infrastructure (no auth server to maintain)
Codemod handles this automatically
ComfyUI integration is confirmed active in Pixelle-Video README -- listed as image/video generation backend
**Dashboard (Next.js 16.1.6 via next-forge) :3000**: Compatible with Node 24 LTS, Bun 1.3.10 as package manager
**Database**: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, MongoDB -- **Prisma adapter confirmed**
**Deferred**: Need to check alternative URLs or GitHub releases for specific breaking changes
Edge-TTS and ComfyUI integrations are prominently featured in README
Edge-TTS is confirmed active -- listed alongside "Index-TTS" as voice synthesis options
Escape: `--webpack` flag to opt out
**Features**: Email/password, 40+ social providers, multi-tenancy/orgs, SSO/SAML/SCIM, 50+ plugins (passkeys, 2FA, magic links), AI agent auth
**Framework support**: First-class Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Astro, Hono, Express, 20+ more
Impact: HIGH -- all dashboard pages using params/searchParams must be updated
Impact: LOW -- dashboard images are mostly UI assets
Impact: LOW-MEDIUM -- if gen1 had middleware, must rename
Impact: LOW -- needs `.eslintrc` -> flat config migration
Impact: LOW -- unless dashboard uses parallel routes
Impact: LOW -- viral-ops unlikely to use AMP or runtime config
Impact: MEDIUM -- any data revalidation code needs updating
Impact: MEDIUM -- viral-ops likely uses no custom webpack, so transparent
Impact: NONE -- gen2 targets Node 24 LTS, React 19.2.4, TS 6.0

**License**: MIT (fully open source)
Migration path exists: replace `@repo/auth` package contents with Better Auth SDK
Mitigation: Codemod available (`@next/codemod upgrade latest`)

n8n docs confirm v2.0 breaking changes exist with a dedicated page and migration tool
next-forge already handles this (built on 16.1.6)
next-forge ships with Clerk pre-integrated via `@repo/auth` workspace package
No API breaking changes since v0.1.15 -- gen1 integration plan remains valid
[NOTE: https://docs.n8n.io/release-notes/2-0-breaking-changes/ returned 404]
**Pixelle-Video v0.1.15 :8000**: Python/FastAPI service, completely independent runtime
**PostgreSQL 18.3 + Prisma 7.4**: Both confirmed compatible (Prisma 7 supports PG 18)
**Recommendation: Start with Clerk** (next-forge default) for these reasons:
Replacing Clerk with Better Auth requires rewiring the auth workspace package -- non-trivial effort
Self-hosted licensing status not confirmed from docs page
**Self-hosted**: Yes -- "Your data stays in your database"

**Stars**: 27.8k GitHub stars, 917 releases, 826+ contributors
Stars: 4.1k, License: Apache 2.0
Supports multiple LLM models: GPT, Qwen, DeepSeek, Ollama
**The 3-service localhost architecture remains valid**
**TypeScript**: Full TypeScript framework ("most comprehensive authentication framework for TypeScript")
**Version**: v1.6.5 (April 16, 2026) -- very actively maintained
**vs Clerk**: Better Auth is OSS/self-hosted (no vendor lock-in, no per-MAU pricing); Clerk is commercial SaaS with free tier but paid for production scale
`async`, `await`, `using` are reserved keywords -- cannot be model names
`Buffer` replaced with `Uint8Array` for Bytes fields
`ExecuteCommand` node: If used, must be explicitly enabled via env var
`fullTextSearch` feature flag must be renamed to `fullTextSearchPostgres` for PostgreSQL
`NotFoundError` removed -- must use `PrismaClientKnownRequestError` with code P2025
**$use() middleware removed**: Must migrate to Client Extensions
**12 environment variables removed**: Including `PRISMA_MIGRATE_SKIP_GENERATE`
**4 nodes removed**: Spontit, Crowd.dev, Kitemaker, Automizy (gen1 does not use these -- no impact)
**Binary data**: Memory mode dropped
**Bun + Node.js coexistence**: next-forge uses Bun for the dashboard; n8n runs on Node.js; no port/runtime conflicts
**CLI changes**: `--skip-generate`, `--skip-seed` removed from `prisma migrate dev`; auto-generation removed; auto-seeding removed
**CLI changes**: `--tunnel` option removed
**ComfyUI/Edge-TTS** (NONE): Pin latest versions, no migration needed
**Config changes**: `N8N_CONFIG_FILES` env var removed
**Driver adapters mandatory**: ALL databases require explicit driver adapters (e.g., `@prisma/adapter-pg` for PostgreSQL)
Environment variable access blocked in code/expressions by default
Environment variable access in expressions: May need to allow explicitly if workflows reference env vars
**Env vars not auto-loaded**: Must manually load `.env` via dotenv
**ESM-only**: Must add `"type": "module"` to package.json, update tsconfig
File access settings enforced by default
**Git node**: Bare repositories disabled by default
HTTP Request nodes calling Pixelle-Video API: **No breaking change** -- HTTP Request node is unchanged
**Import path changes**: From `@prisma/client` to custom generated paths (e.g., `./generated/prisma/client`)
**Key recent features**: LTX2 audio, Ace Step 1.5 XL, RT-DETRv4 detection, flux 2 decoder, fp16 optimizations, VAE VRAM improvements
**Latest release**: v0.19.0 (April 13, 2025)
**Latest version**: 7.2.8 (March 22, 2026)
**License**: GPL-3.0 (unchanged)
**License**: LGPLv3
**Major version jump**: Was likely ~6.x at gen1 time, now 7.2.8 -- but the API (`edge-tts` CLI and Python module) has remained stable
M:N relation tables (if any in gen1) will get auto-migrated primary keys
**MySQL/MariaDB dropped**: Only PostgreSQL and SQLite supported now (gen1 uses PostgreSQL -- no impact)
**n8n 1->2** (LOW): Mostly security hardening; core workflow format unchanged
**n8n 2.x + PostgreSQL 18.3**: n8n supports PG as primary backend; PG 18 is standard
**n8n HTTP Request -> Pixelle-Video**: HTTP Request node API unchanged in n8n 2.0; same REST calls work
**New provider**: `prisma-client-js` removed, must use `prisma-client`
**Next.js 14->16** (MEDIUM): Async APIs, Turbopack default, middleware changes (documented in iteration 4)
**No breaking API changes** for Pixelle-Video integration -- ComfyUI's workflow JSON format remains stable across 0.x releases
**No breaking changes** for Pixelle-Video's TTS integration
Node.js 18.18.0+ required (gen1 used 18+, so OK if pinned correctly)
Node.js 20.19.0+ required (gen2 targets Node 24, so OK)
**Nodes disabled by default**: `ExecuteCommand` and `LocalFileTrigger` must be explicitly enabled
**No version combination breaks the gen1 architecture**
OAuth callback endpoints require authentication
**Output field mandatory**: Prisma Client no longer generates to node_modules; must specify output path
**Pin recommendation**: v0.19.0 or latest (Pixelle-Video bundles its own ComfyUI workflows)
**PostgreSQL 18.3 + Prisma 7.4**: Compatible via `@prisma/adapter-pg` driver adapter (mandatory in v7)
PostgreSQL backend: **No breaking change** -- PG still fully supported
PostgreSQL M:N relation tables change from unique index to primary key (auto-migration generated)
**Previous**: 7.2.7 (December 2025)
**Prisma 5->7** (HIGH): ESM migration, driver adapters, prisma.config.ts, import path changes
**prisma.config.ts mandatory**: Configuration centralized in TypeScript config file at project root
**Pyodide disabled**: Python execution via Pyodide made unrunnable
**Python requirement**: >=3.7
**Release cadence**: Very active -- v0.16 through v0.19 in roughly one month (March-April 2025)
Schema itself likely compatible (no JSONB-specific breaks found, UUID generation unchanged)
**Security hardening**:
Self-hosted licensing: Release notes do not mention licensing changes -- still free for self-host
**SQLite**: Non-pooling driver dropped
**SSL validation strict by default**: May need explicit config for local dev
**Status**: Actively maintained, no deprecation notices
**Thai voices**: Still available (th-TH-PremwadeeNeural, th-TH-NiwatNeural, th-TH-AcharaNeural) -- Microsoft Edge TTS service remains operational
The BIGGEST migration effort is the ESM + driver adapter + prisma.config.ts restructuring, not the schema
TypeScript 5.1.0+ required
TypeScript 5.4.0+ required (gen2 targets TS 6.0, so OK)
**Versioning**: Semantic versioning, still pre-1.0 (v0.x.x)
**Worker changes**: Implicit retries setting removed

ruledOutDirections.text: Checking Auth.js, ShadCN UI, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, ComfyUI, Edge-TTS, Pixelle-Video versions (not enough tool budget this iteration -- deferred)
Checking individual component changelogs in detail (deferred to focused iterations per component)
None identified this iteration (first survey pass)
**Makerkit, SaaS-Starter-Kit, SaaSBold, Shipfast**: Not investigated this iteration -- defer to iteration 3 if next-forge doesn't pan out
**npm direct scraping for Tremor**: Returns 403, need alternative approach (GitHub API or registry.npmjs.org API)
**taxonomy (shadcn/taxonomy)**: Not investigated this iteration due to tool budget -- defer to iteration 3
**tremor-raw as alternative to Tremor**: Confirmed dead. Only 2 commits, redirects to main tremor repo. Not a viable separate path.

**Auth.js v5 as auth solution**: Auth.js has been absorbed into "Better Auth" project. The authjs.dev site confirms this. Auth.js v5 as a standalone maintained project is effectively deprecated. This eliminates Auth.js as a direct dependency choice -- must either adopt Better Auth, use Clerk (next-forge default), or evaluate alternatives.

Checking individual next-forge workspace packages (e.g., `@repo/auth`, `@repo/design-system`) for exact Clerk/Prisma versions -- deferred due to tool budget. The web app package.json confirmed the framework versions which was the priority.
Direct npm registry scraping (already BLOCKED from iteration 2)
Auth.js v5 as auth solution (already BLOCKED from iteration 3 -- confirmed again: Better Auth is the successor)
n8n v2.0 breaking changes at `docs.n8n.io/release-notes/2-0-breaking-changes/` (404 -- URL restructured)
None this iteration. The n8n URL 404 is a URL issue, not a fundamental dead end -- alternative URLs exist.
Direct Prisma 5->7 upgrade: Must go through 6 first (two-stage migration)
n8n docs.n8n.io breaking changes URL: Already blocked from iteration 4; used GitHub releases instead (success)
None new this iteration. All research avenues were productive.
